PlayerProfile.title: PlayerProfile
PlayerProfile.description: Represents a player's core persistent data and game-related profile information, excluding authentication details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerProfile entity.

createdDate.description: Timestamp when the player's profile was created.

lastLoginDate.description: Timestamp of the player's last login.

currencyBalance.description: The player's balance of in-game currency.

experiencePoints.description: The total experience points accumulated by the player.

agentIds.description: References to Agents owned by this player. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Agent)

activeQuestIds.description: References to Quests currently active for this player. (Relationship: PlayerProfile N:N Quest)

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an autonomous entity within the game world, which can be player-controlled or an NPC performing actions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the PlayerProfile if this is a player-controlled agent. Null if an independent NPC. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Agent)

currentPosition.description: The agent's current coordinates or location identifier in the game world (e.g., 'x,y,z').

health.description: The current health points of the agent.

mana.description: The current mana or energy points of the agent.

combatSkill.description: The agent's skill level in combat.

gatheringSkill.description: The agent's skill level in gathering resources.

tradeSkill.description: The agent's skill level in trading.

explorationSkill.description: The agent's skill level in exploration.

currentAction.description: The action the agent is currently performing (e.g., 'idle', 'gathering', 'combat', 'trading', 'exploring').

inventoryItemIds.description: References to Items currently in the agent's inventory. (Relationship: Agent N:N Item)

Civilization.title: Civilization
Civilization.description: Represents a distinct civilization within the game world, including its aggregated index and characteristics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Civilization entity.

civilizationIndex.description: A calculated index representing the overall development and influence of the civilization.

description.description: A descriptive text about the civilization.

foundingDate.description: The date when the civilization was founded or first recorded.

leaderName.description: The name of the current leader of the civilization.

territoryDescription.description: A description or coordinates outlining the territory controlled by the civilization.

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Represents a quest or mission available to players, potentially generated dynamically by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the quest, its objectives, and lore.

generatedByAI.description: Indicates if this quest was dynamically generated by an AI.

status.description: The current global status of the quest (e.g., 'available', 'unavailable', 'active', 'completed').

difficulty.description: The estimated difficulty level of the quest (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'hard').

rewards.description: A JSON string or descriptive text outlining the rewards for completing the quest.

requirements.description: A JSON string or descriptive text outlining the conditions or prerequisites to start the quest.

relatedNPCId.description: Reference to the NPC associated with this quest, if any. (Relationship: NPC 1:N Quest)

scriptContent.description: Generated script content (e.g., YouTube script) related to the quest's narrative or an NPC's dialogue.

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Represents a Non-Player Character in the game world, potentially with AI-generated personalities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

personalityDescription.description: An AI-generated description of the NPC's personality traits and quirks.

dialogueTree.description: A JSON string representing the NPC's dialogue options and conversational flow.

factionId.description: Reference to the Faction this NPC belongs to. (Relationship: Faction 1:N NPC)

currentLocation.description: The NPC's current coordinates or location identifier in the game world (e.g., 'x,y,z').

questIds.description: References to Quests that this NPC is involved with or offers. (Relationship: NPC N:N Quest)

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Represents a distinct item or type of loot that can exist in the game world, in inventories, or as rewards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

name.description: The display name of the item.

description.description: A descriptive text about the item's properties and lore.

rarity.description: The rarity level of the item (e.g., 'common', 'uncommon', 'rare', 'legendary').

value.description: The inherent value of the item in the game's internal currency.

gameAssetId.description: Reference to the GameAsset representing the visual model or icon of this item. (Relationship: Item 1:N GameAsset)

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an item or bundle available for purchase in the Axiom Storefront with real or in-game currency.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product as displayed in the storefront.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

priceRealCurrency.description: The price of the product in real-world currency.

priceGameCurrency.description: The price of the product in in-game currency, if applicable.

itemIds.description: References to Items included in this product bundle. (Relationship: Product N:N Item)

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the product.

availableFrom.description: The date and time from which the product is available for purchase.

availableTo.description: The date and time until which the product is available for purchase.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Records a transaction made by a player in the Axiom Storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the PlayerProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

currencyUsed.description: The type of currency used for the transaction (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'AXIOM_COIN').

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'failed', 'refunded').

transactionId.description: The unique identifier provided by the payment gateway (e.g., PayPal).

productIds.description: References to Products included in this order. (Relationship: Order N:N Product)

GameAsset.title: GameAsset
GameAsset.description: Manages in-game assets and textures for the Godot client, stored in Firebase Storage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameAsset entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the asset.

storagePath.description: The Firebase Storage URI or path where the asset file is located.

version.description: The version identifier for the asset, allowing for updates without breaking references.

checksum.description: A checksum (e.g., MD5, SHA-256) for verifying the integrity of the downloaded asset file.

description.description: A brief description of the asset's purpose or visual characteristics.

WorldParameter.title: WorldParameter
WorldParameter.description: Represents global game settings or configuration parameters that can be managed via the Admin Console.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldParameter entity (e.g., 'CIV_INDEX_WEIGHT', 'LOOT_DROP_RATE_MULTIPLIER').

name.description: A human-readable name for the parameter.

value.description: The current value of the parameter, stored as a string to accommodate various data types (numbers, booleans, JSON).

type.description: The expected data type of the parameter's value (e.g., 'number', 'string', 'boolean', 'json').

description.description: A detailed explanation of what the parameter controls.

lastUpdatedBy.description: The ID of the administrator or system process that last updated this parameter.

lastUpdatedDate.description: Timestamp of the last update to this parameter.

definition.description: Core data for a player. Owned by the user whose UID matches the document ID. Includes denormalized 'id' matching the path's '{playerId}' for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the player, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Agents owned by a specific player. The 'playerId' field in the Agent document must match the path's '{playerId}' for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owning player.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the agent.

definition.description: Purchase orders made by a specific player. The 'playerId' field in the Order document must match the path's '{playerId}' for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the player who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order.

definition.description: Global game civilizations. Publicly readable, writeable only by administrators.

params.name: civilizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the civilization.

definition.description: Global quest definitions. Publicly readable, writeable only by administrators or AI services. Player-specific quest progress/status would typically be tracked within a player's private data.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quest.

definition.description: Global Non-Player Characters. Publicly readable for interaction in the game world, writeable only by administrators or AI services.

params.name: npcId
params.description: The unique identifier for the NPC.

definition.description: Global catalog of game items. Publicly readable (e.g., for inventories or store displays), writeable only by administrators.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the item.

definition.description: Catalog of storefront products. Publicly readable for the Axiom Storefront, writeable only by administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Metadata for in-game assets stored in Firebase Storage. Publicly readable for the Godot client, writeable only by administrators.

params.name: gameAssetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the game asset.

definition.description: Global game configuration parameters. Readable by game logic (Cloud Functions) and administrators. Writeable only by administrators.

params.name: parameterId
params.description: The unique identifier for the world parameter (e.g., 'CIV_INDEX_WEIGHT').

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and QAPs (Queryable Authorization Patterns) to ensure a secure, scalable, and debuggable system for Axiom Frontier. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Mandate A):**
This design explicitly denormalizes authorization context for player-owned subcollections. For entities like `Agent` and `Order`, which are owned by a `PlayerProfile`, the `playerId` is copied directly into each `Agent` and `Order` document. For example, documents in `/playerProfiles/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}` will contain a `playerId` field matching the `{playerId}` wildcard in the path. This eliminates the need for `get()` operations in Firestore Security Rules to check parent documents, making rules atomic, simpler, and less prone to performance issues or deadlocks in batched writes/transactions. 

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) (Mandate B & C):**

1.  **Private Player Data:** Player-specific entities (`PlayerProfile`, `Agent`, `Order`) are organized under hierarchical paths: 
    *   `/playerProfiles/{playerId}`: A top-level collection where each document's ID (`playerId`) is the Firebase Authentication UID of the owner. Access is restricted to `request.auth.uid == playerId` for the document itself.
    *   `/playerProfiles/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}`: A subcollection storing `Agent` documents. Security rules for this path will check that `request.auth.uid == playerId` (from the path) and also confirm `resource.data.playerId == playerId` (from the denormalized field), ensuring that a player can only access their own agents. This structure guarantees that `list` queries from the client will inherently filter by the authenticated user's ID, thus satisfying the QAP principle.
    *   `/playerProfiles/{playerId}/orders/{orderId}`: Similar to `agents`, this subcollection stores `Order` documents. Rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == playerId` and `resource.data.playerId == playerId`, allowing players to only `list` or `get` their own orders.

2.  **Global/Public Data:** Entities like `Civilization`, `Quest`, `NPC`, `Item`, `Product`, `GameAsset`, and `WorldParameter` reside in top-level collections. These collections generally have a 'public read' security posture for the game client or specific services, with 'admin write' access. `list` operations on these collections are naturally queryable as they expose data intended for broad consumption.

3.  **DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):** For administrative roles, a separate collection (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`) would be used to store the UIDs of authorized administrators. Security rules would then check for the existence of `request.auth.uid` in this collection to grant elevated permissions for managing global data collections (`civilizations`, `quests`, `npcs`, `items`, `products`, `gameAssets`, `worldParameters`).

This design ensures that security rules are straightforward, directly mapping to the data structure without complex `get()` calls, enabling efficient queries and atomic operations while maintaining strict access control for player data and global game parameters.
